zh.lifeSummary: 當Eleanor Shottesbroke於1421年出生在英國英格蘭波克夏韋斯特 武德海時，她的父親Robert Shottesbroke11418歲，母親Edith Stourton42歲。她和Sir John Cheney至少有4個兒子和5個女兒。她去世於1507年11月在英國英格蘭肯特肯特享年86歲。

nameMeanings.name: Eleanor
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: From an Old French respelling of the Old Provençal name Alienor. This has sometimes been taken as a derivative of Helen , but it is more probably of Germanic derivation (the first element being ali ‘other, foreign’ the second is obscure). The name was introduced to England by Eleanor of Aquitaine ( 1122–1204 ), who came from Aquitaine in south-west France to be the wife of King Henry II . It was also borne by Eleanor of Provence, the wife of Henry III , and Eleanor of Castile, wife of Edward I .

variants.name: Ellenor
meanings: Variant spelling of   Eleanor  , the result of blending with   Ellen .

variants.name: Elenor
meanings: From an Old French respelling of the Old Provençal name Alienor. This has sometimes been taken as a derivative of Helen , but it is more probably of Germanic derivation (the first element being ali ‘other, foreign’ the second is obscure). The name was introduced to England by Eleanor of Aquitaine ( 1122–1204 ), who came from Aquitaine in south-west France to be the wife of King Henry II . It was also borne by Eleanor of Provence, the wife of Henry III , and Eleanor of Castile, wife of Edward I .

en.lifeSummary: When Eleanor Shottesbroke was born in 1421, in West Woodhay, Berkshire, England, United Kingdom, her father, Robert Shottesbroke, was 11418 and her mother, Edith Stourton, was 42. She had at least 4 sons and 5 daughters with Sir John Cheney. She died in November 1507, in Kent, Kent, England, United Kingdom, at the age of 86.

ko.lifeSummary: Eleanor Shottesbroke(이)가 1421년 영국 잉글랜드 버크실르 웨스트 우드하이에서 태어났을 때, 그녀의 아버지 Robert Shottesbroke(은)는 11418세였고, 그녀의 어머니 Edith Stourton(은)는 42세였다. 그녀는 Sir John Cheney(와)과 적어도 네 명의 아들과 다섯 명의 딸을 두었다. 그녀는 1507년 11월 영국 잉글랜드 켄트 켄트에서 86세의 나이로 사망했다.
